reason_for_decision: The article passes because it explains false-alarm reduction as a workflow and triage design problem rather than as a simple threshold-tuning exercise.
strengths: The article clearly separates cheap nuisance signals from expensive nuisance work, which gives the topic a stronger operational frame than generic alarm-tuning advice.
It adds useful decision value by explaining context, corroboration, verification ladders, and measurement design in one coherent workflow.
The measurement section is practical and keeps the argument grounded in latency and leakage rather than in vague claims about improvement.
summary_assessment: This is a strong professional knowledge-base article. It is clear, disciplined, and useful for teams trying to reduce nuisance burden without quietly damaging response performance.
